cards.question: What's wrong with this program that won't compile?
cards.explanation: Every statement in C++ must end with a semicolon. The return statement is missing its semicolon before the closing brace.

cards.text: One of the most common beginner mistakes is forgetting the {{c1::semicolon}} at the end of a {{c2::statement}}.

cards.question: Why does this code produce a compiler error?
cards.explanation: C++ is case-sensitive. Keywords like 'return', 'int', 'if' must be lowercase. 'Return' is treated as an undefined identifier.

cards.back: Yes. C++ is fully case-sensitive. 'main', 'Main', and 'MAIN' are three different identifiers. Keywords must be lowercase.

cards.question: What's the problem with this code?
cards.answer: Using parentheses ( ) instead of curly braces { } for the function body
cards.explanation: Function bodies must be enclosed in curly braces { }, not parentheses. Parentheses are used for function parameters.

cards.concept: How to read and fix compiler errors effectively
cards.explanation:
Compiler errors can be intimidating but follow a pattern:
  • Find the line number where the error occurred
  • Read the error message (even if confusing)
  • Look for common issues: missing semicolons, typos, case errors
  • Fix the FIRST error first (later errors may cascade)
  • Check the line mentioned AND the line before it
examples: error C2143: missing ';' before '}' → check for missing semicolon
error: 'Return' was not declared → check capitalization
error: expected ';' before 'std' → previous line missing semicolon

cards.text: When you have multiple compiler errors, fix the {{c1::first}} error first, as later errors may {{c2::cascade}} from earlier ones.

cards.front: Why might a compiler error point to the wrong line?
cards.back: The error might actually be on a previous line (like a missing semicolon). The compiler only detects the error when it reaches a point where the syntax doesn't make sense.

cards.question: What will happen when this code runs?
cards.answer: Undefined behavior (may work or may not). Main should return an int value.
cards.explanation: The main function is declared to return int, but doesn't return a value. This is technically undefined behavior, though many compilers implicitly return 0.

cards.front: What should you do if you can't understand a compiler error message?
cards.back: Copy the error message and search online (Google, Stack Overflow). Many compiler errors are common, and others have encountered and explained them.

cards.explanation:
Best practices to avoid syntax errors:
  • Type carefully and check for typos
  • Use an IDE with syntax highlighting
  • Let IDE auto-complete identifiers
  • Compile frequently (after small changes)
  • Fix errors immediately before writing more code
  • Use IDE auto-formatting to spot mismatched braces
examples: Use IDE's 'format document' to check brace matching
